title: Bind mount in privileged machine are not owned by root
body:
### Issue Description

While using bind mount in a container as part of a podman rootfull machine, folder content is owned by "501" user,  that matches - in my case - the user id executing the podman machine/container. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
cd ~

podman run \
  --rm \
  -v ./:/app \             
  alpine:latest \
  ls -la /app
```

### Describe the results you received

total 130560
drwxr-x---   94 501      dialout       3008 Dec 21 11:02 .
dr-xr-xr-x    1 root     root            39 Dec 21 16:36 ..
-r--------    1 501      dialout          7 Sep 19  2022 [omitted]

### Describe the results you expected

total 130560
drwxr-x---   94 root      root       3008 Dec 21 11:02 .
dr-xr-xr-x    1 root     root            39 Dec 21 16:36 ..
-r--------    1 root      root          7 Sep 19  2022 [omitted]

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Apple silicon (M1) processor

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@glm-bloomreach Can you retry with podman 5.0?

@baude @ashley-cui PTAL I assume this is somewhat expected, not sure what the best fix here is. Can we chown files now with virtiofs or is the VM still limited to only one uid/gid set at mount time?
